item_bonus_note: Item bonus to Recall Knowledge regardless of the skill
markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 20"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Whispering Staff](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4679)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Apex" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=10" />
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Staff" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=151" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 105

**Price** 70,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This gnarled wooden staff is carved with humanoid faces in various emotional states. When the staff is activated, the faces begin to whisper a variety of languages in sibilant tones, creating what seems to be nonsense to all but the staff’s wielder or those they choose to affect. The staff functions as a [_major staff of divination_](/Equipment.aspx?ID=3048). While using the staff, you gain a +3 item bonus to [Decipher Writing](/Actions.aspx?ID=2363), [Identify Magic](/Actions.aspx?ID=2365), and [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=2367) checks, regardless of the skill. When you invest the staff, you either increase your Intelligence score by 2 or increase it to 18, whichever is higher. You must select the skills and languages the first time you invest the item, and whenever you invest the same whispering staff, you get the same skills and languages you chose the first time.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> envision ([mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) **Frequency** once per round;   **Effect** Through the staff’s strange whispering, you gain a glimpse into the mind and desires of one creature you can see within 30 feet. Until the end of your next turn, that creature is [flat-footed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=58) to you and takes a –2 circumstance penalty to saving throws against your spells.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> Interact ([mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647), [misfortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=654)) **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** You point the staff at one creature you can see within 30 feet of you, causing the whispers to howl in that creature’s mind. The target must attempt a DC 43 Will save. If it fails, whenever the creature attempts an attack roll, skill check, or saving throw, it must roll twice and take the worse roll. This lasts until the start of your next turn.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> envision ([fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=612), [mental](/Traits.aspx?ID=647)) **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You twirl the staff in three consecutive circles and call for the whispers to speak up. For the next minute, you and all allies within a 30-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) around you can hear your staff’s whispers clearly and distinctly, gaining benefit from their advice and mental protection. Whenever you and your affected allies attempt to [Recall Knowledge](/Actions.aspx?ID=2367) or attempt a saving throw against a mental effect, you roll twice and take the better result. This is a [fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=612) effect.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Whispering Staff

item_bonus_note: Item bonus to Performance checks made with the instrument
markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Confabulator](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4700)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Illusion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=92" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 109

**Price** 55 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** Affixed to instrument

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This device attaches to any non-percussive musical instrument, allowing a non-verbal character to shape the sounds of the instrument into speech. The speech can be any language the character understands, and the sound of the speech resembles the instrument the device is attached to. You also gain a +1 item bonus to [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) checks made with the instrument.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Confabulator

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 8"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Chair of Inventions](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4701)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 109

**Price** 875 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This wheelchair is outfitted with a variety of tools and devices to assist with the creation and production of a number of mechanical implements. While seated in a chair of inventions, you have a worn [superb repair kit](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2747) that doesn’t count against your Bulk limit or maximum worn items.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> command, Interact;  **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** The chair deploys a complete e[xpanded alchemist’s lab](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2701). The chair is immobile while this lab is deployed, but levers and gears in the chair allow you to easily retrieve and access everything you need from both the attached superb repair kit and the deployed lab to [Craft](/Actions.aspx?ID=2385). This setup is highly efficient and gives you a +2 circumstance bonus to [Earn Income](/Actions.aspx?ID=2364) using [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

</title>

<row gap="medium">

**Bulk** L
</row>

---

This short bronze rod has the form of a serpent coiled around it. While you hold it, you gain a +1 item bonus to [Medicine](/Skills.aspx?ID=42) checks.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> command, Interact;  **Frequency** once per hour;  **Requirements** You have the [Battle Medicine](/Feats.aspx?ID=5125) action; **Effect** You use Battle Medicine. The target is temporarily immune to your Battle Medicine for 1 hour instead of 1 day.

</column>

item_bonus_note: Item bonus to sight-based Perception
markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 12"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Bring Me Near](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4722)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Teleportation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=156" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 114

**Price** 1,800 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This collapsible fine spyglass consists of 3 leather tubes that slide into one another. The edge of each is trimmed in silver, and the lenses are made of finely crafted glass. While looking through it, you gain a +2 item bonus to any [Perception](/Rules.aspx?ID=2040) checks made involving sight.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> 1 minute (envision, Interact);  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You focus on any spot you can see within 5 miles through the spyglass and rotate its parts in a meticulous order. You and up to 4 willing creatures adjacent to you are instantly teleported to that spot. If there’s not enough room for everyone, only you are transported. If there’s not enough room for you, the teleportation fails.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Bring Me Near

item_bonus_note: Item bonus to Survival checks
markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Sextant of the Night](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4730)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 116

**Price** 95 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This finely wrought sextant is made from silver with several onyx mirrors and shades made from thin sets of crystal glass. A fine spyglass made of silver is affixed to the frame; removing the spyglass destroys the sextant. By all appearances, the sextant shouldn’t function, as the shades and mirrors are swapped, but when you look through the spyglass, you see a night sky during the day and the sun during the night, as if day and night were inverted. 

When you use the sextant, you gain a +1 item bonus to [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) checks, which increases to a +2 item bonus when you [Sense Direction](/Actions.aspx?ID=2406).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Sextant of the Night

item_bonus_note: Item bonus to Intimidation and Initiative checks
markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 10"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Standard of the Primeval Howl](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4731)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Enchantment" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=61" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Mental" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=106" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 116

**Price** 900 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 1 hand

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These standards are always constructed from uncut wood and a leather banner painted with the visage of a snarling beast—a wolf, boar, bear, lion, dragon, or similarly imposing creature. And while it might become lost on a battlefield scattered with gaudier standards, its effect bolsters those around a competent leader. When carrying this banner, you gain a +1 item bonus to [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) checks and initiative rolls, and creatures in a 20-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) also gain a +1 item bonus to initiative checks.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> command;  **Frequency** once per hour; **Trigger** An ally within 20 feet of you critically hits with a [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=2306); **Requirements** You have the [Battle Cry](/Feats.aspx?ID=5124) skill feat; **Effect** You attempt to [Demoralize](/Actions.aspx?ID=2395) the foe the Strike hit.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Standard of the Primeval Howl

item_bonus_note: Item bonus to Performance checks made with the instrument
markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 9"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Violin of the Waves](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4733)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Auditory" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=16" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 116

**Price** 700 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** held in 2 hands

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This violin is crafted from finely wrought rosewood that emits a strong, but pleasant, smell of salt water and ocean life. It’s engraved with images of sailors working and waves gently rolling. When you make a [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) check using the _violin of the waves_, you gain a +2 item bonus to the check. This bonus increases to +3 if the performer is currently aboard a ship, walking on the ocean, or otherwise immediately adjacent to ocean water.

The violin was created to play a specific tune, one that springs instantly into the mind of anyone who so much as casually strums the instrument. It’s a tune of calm waters and safety, but also of pranks played upon shipmates and revelry.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> 5 minutes (Interact);  **Frequency** once per day;  **Requirements** You must be aboard a ship; **Effect** You play the song. Once it’s completed, the weather immediately calms to the normal as it would for the season, as control weather. For the next day, the weather remains in this state, unless affected by other magical effects. Anyone aboard the ship finds their mind wanders when performing tasks however, daydreaming of drunken revelry or other forms of entertainment, and the crew of the ship takes a –2 status penalty to skill checks to do anything other than participate in such revelry.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Violin of the Waves

item_bonus_note: Item bonus to a particular Lore check
markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Ancestral Geometry](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4736)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Abjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=2" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Tattoo" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=262" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 119

**Price** 30 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** tattooed on the body

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Geometric precision and perfect angles signify that an artist with exemplary knowledge of [dwarven](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=59) tattooing traditions created this body art. Your family’s ancestral stories, recited throughout the tattooing process, bind your blood even tighter to theirs. During your daily preparations, you manifest a visitation by an ancestor—possibly via a dream, a vision, or a magical trinket left by your bedroll. Roll 2d20 and record the highest result. Then roll 1d6 and note a type of saving throw: 1–2 Fortitude, 3–4 Reflex, and 5–6 Will.

In addition, until the next visitation, you gain a +1 item bonus to one Lore skill related to the ancestor, as determined by the GM. Usually, the ancestor provides a kind of knowledge they believe you’ll need. For dwarven ancestors, the Lore skill is usually [Architecture Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41), Engineering Lore, Genealogy Lore, Labor Lore, Mining Lore, Warfare Lore, or Lore about a dwarven deity.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> envision ([fortune](/Traits.aspx?ID=612)) **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You rolled a saving throw of the noted type;  **Effect** Replace the roll with the d20 roll from your ancestor’s visitation.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Ancestral Geometry

Small scars or marks around your eye improve your distant vision. These scars are especially common among [orc](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=66) scouts, who favor scars shaped like eagle talons. Higher-level versions of an eye slash are larger and more elaborate scars or marks, radiating out around the eye.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[One Hundred Victories](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4748)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Necromancy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=117" />
<trait label="Tattoo" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=262" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 121

**Price** 60 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** tattooed on the body

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

One hundred cuts, healed into diamond-shaped scars, represent the ability to withstand the attacks of your enemies. [Orc](/Ancestries.aspx?ID=66) warriors covet these scar patterns and cluster them around what they consider to be their strongest assets—a pattern around the heart signifies a warrior with great endurance, while one along the arms indicates great upper body strength. 

You gain a +1 item bonus to [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) checks. If you have Ferocity (such as from the Orc Ferocity feat or a similar ability), the first time each day you use it, instead of remaining at 1 Hit Point, your Hit Points are set to an amount equal to your ancestry Hit Points, even if this amount is more than you had before using [Ferocity](/MonsterAbilities.aspx?ID=63).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: One Hundred Victories

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 9+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Tradecraft Tattoo](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4754)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Tattoo" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=262" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 122

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** tattooed on the body

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Crafters choose tattoos that represent their dedication and skill in their chosen field. Such tattoos might adorn the arm, fingers, or eyes, and they take the form of artistic patterns or depict tools of the trade, such as anvils, paintbrushes, or trowels. You gain a +2 item bonus to [Crafting](/Skills.aspx?ID=37) checks. Furthermore, when you roll a critical failure on a Crafting check to [Earn Income](/Actions.aspx?ID=2364), treat it as a failure instead.

**Activate** <actions string="" /> 1 minute (envision, Interact);  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The tattoo casts [_creation_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1477). You choose the item and its appearance, and whether the spell is 4th or 5th level.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

pfs="Standard"

</row>

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 2"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Triangular Teeth](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4755)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Abjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=2" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Tattoo" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=262" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 122

**Price** 33 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** tattooed on the body

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Rows of triangles symbolizing shark teeth protect you from danger and enable you to take fierce retaliation against those who try to harm you. Seafarers, especially those on the seas of Minata, wear these tattoos in patterns, with multiple rows of regular triangles. You gain a +1 item bonus to [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) checks to navigate bodies of water.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> command;  **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You would be hit by an attack against your AC;  **Effect** You gain a +1 circumstance bonus to AC against the attack, or a +2 circumstance bonus if the attacker is in water or has the [amphibious](/Traits.aspx?ID=529), [aquatic](/Traits.aspx?ID=533), or [water](/Traits.aspx?ID=732) trait. Whether the attack hits or misses, you gain a +2 status bonus to damage with the next [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=1167) you make against the attacker before the end of your next turn.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Triangular Teeth

markdown:
**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 122 **Price** 60 gp **Usage** tattooed on the body --- The name of your beloved adorns this stylized image of a heart. An unbreakable heart tattoo can be received only at the end of a successful [_heartbond_](/Rituals.aspx?ID=149) ritual, serving as your token. The other participant can have a token other than a tattoo if they wish. If you have more than one _heartbond_, each unbreakable heart you have serves as a token for only one of them. The love exuding from you grants you a +1 item bonus to [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) checks. When you use heartbond’s activity to learn your beloved’s present state, you can also grant them a small gift, choosing from the following options each time. Make your choice after learning their state.
  • Your beloved gains 5 [temporary Hit Points](/Rules.aspx?ID=2321) that last for 10 minutes.
  • The tattoo casts [_guidance_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1549) on your beloved.
  • The tattoo casts [_stabilize_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1689) on your beloved.
  • The tattoo casts [_light_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1585) on your beloved’s token from _heartbond_.
name: Unbreakable Heart

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Varisian Emblem](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4757)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Tattoo" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=262" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 123

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** tattooed on the body

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These tattoos are created using designs and techniques from Varisian culture. Such tattoos often depict the Varisian rune for one of the seven schools of magic, but one might instead show a creature or celestial phenomenon associated with the school, depicted in traditional Varisian iconography. The tattoo allows you to understand and speak Varisian. If you already know that language, you instead gain a +1 item bonus on [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) checks you make when speaking [Varisian](/Languages.aspx?ID=174) to someone who understands it.

Each description lists the school of each Varisian emblem, and the tattoo has the trait of that school. The spell the tattoo can cast is from its school. As with the Thassilonian magic that was once so prevalent in Varisia, these tattoos lack a symbol of [divination](/Traits.aspx?ID=47).

**Activate** <actions string="" /> The actions required to Activate the tattoo are the same as those needed to cast its spell;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The tattoo casts its spell.

</column>

item_bonus_note: Item bonus to Stealth Checks
markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 4+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Wildwood Ink](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4758)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Primal" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=134" />
<trait label="Tattoo" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=262" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 123

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** tattooed on the body

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These curving, delicate designs resemble leaves, vines, or creepers, most often wrapped around a limb, ear, or throat, or curled around specific muscles. They help you blend in among plants. You gain a +1 item bonus to [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) checks, which increases to +2 in forests.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> ;  **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** A creature would detect you by Seeking; **Requirements** You’re in a forest or similar natural area; **Effect** The tattoo casts tree shape on you before you can be noticed. The duration of this spell is 10 minutes.

If you’ve already Activated the tattoo, you can supply a separate casting of [_tree shape_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1618) to recharge the tattoo instead of having the spell’s normal effect. This allows you to Activate the tattoo again in the same day. You can do so multiple times each day, but only as many times as you continue to cast tree shape to recharge the tattoo after each use.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 4"
pfs="Standard"
>
Wildwood Ink
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 123

**Price** 80 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

<title
level="2"
right="Item 10"
pfs="Standard"
>
Wildwood Ink (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 123

**Price** 900 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The item bonus to Stealth checks is +2, or +3 in forests. Tree shape lasts up to 1 hour.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 17"
pfs="Standard"
>
Wildwood Ink (Major)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 123

**Price** 15,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The item bonus to Stealth checks is +3, or +4 in forests. Tree shape lasts up to 8 hours.
name: Wildwood Ink

The duration of this spell is 10 minutes.

If you’ve already Activated the tattoo, you can supply a separate casting of [_tree shape_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1618) to recharge the tattoo instead of having the spell’s normal effect. This allows you to Activate the tattoo again in the same day. You can do so multiple times each day, but only as many times as you continue to cast tree shape to recharge the tattoo after each use.

item_bonus_note: Item bonus to Trip when affixed to a melee weapon
markdown:
**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 124 **Usage** affixed to armor or weapon --- This silver disc displays an ever-changing etching of an animal. When you affix the _beastmaster’s sigil_, the animal settles into a single form based on where you affix it, showing the animal the item can summon when affixed that way. The spell DC of any spell cast by activating this item is 19. A weapon can benefit from only the melee weapon benefit or ranged weapon benefit. A thrown melee weapon gets the melee weapon benefit, and a combination weapon gets only one benefit, which you choose when you affix the spellheart. If it’s unclear which one should apply, the GM decides.
  • **Armor** The sigil grants you a +1 item bonus to saving throws against poison.
  • **Melee Weapo**n The sigil grants you a +1 item bonus to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) checks to [Trip](/Actions.aspx?ID=2382).
  • **Ranged Weapon** If you critically hit with the affixed weapon, and the target is adjacent to a creature summoned with the sigil, the target takes 1d6 [persistent bleed damage](/Conditions.aspx?ID=86).
**Activate** Cast a Spell; **Effect** You cast [_tame_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1014). Beastmaster's Sigil **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 124 **Price** 160 gp --- **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast 3rd-level _summon animal_ to summon a [giant monitor lizard](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3088) (armor), [wolverine](/Monsters.aspx?ID=862) (melee), or [giant bat](/Monsters.aspx?ID=2849) (ranged). Beastmaster's Sigil (Greater) **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 124 **Price** 360 gp --- The spell DC is 23 and the bleed damage is 2d6. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast 4th-level _summon animal_ to summon a [giant scorpion](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3175) (armor), [dire wolf](/Monsters.aspx?ID=3242) (melee), or [fen mosquito swarm](/Monsters.aspx?ID=732) (ranged). Beastmaster's Sigil (Major) **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 125 **Price** 700 gp --- The spell DC is 25, the item bonuses are +2, and the bleed damage is 3d6. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Effect** You cast [_tanglefoot_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1713).
name: Beastmaster's Sigil

item_bonus_note: Item bonus to Create a Diversion checks
markdown:
**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 127 **Usage** affixed to armor or weapon --- The first of these strips of glittering cloth was worn by a monk from Jinin who would interweave it into his handwraps of mighty blows. The spell DC of any spell cast by activating this item is 24.
  • **Armor** You gain a +2 item bonus to saving throws against [illusions](/ArcaneSchools.aspx?ID=6) and [Deception](/Skills.aspx?ID=38) checks to [Create a Diversion](/Actions.aspx?ID=2387).
  • **Weapon** ([visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727)) After you cast an illusion spell by activating the scarf, the weapon is shrouded in a mesmerizing illusory pattern. Your next Strike causes the target to be [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=63) for 1 round if it hits. If you don’t make a Strike by the end of your next turn, the illusion ends with no effect.
**Activate** Cast a Spell; **Effect** You cast [_dancing lights_](/Spells.aspx?ID=58). Lightweave Scarf **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 127 **Price** 500 gp --- **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast _[_hypnotic pattern_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1564)_. Lightweave Scarf (Greater) **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 127 **Price** 5,500 gp --- **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast 6th-level _hypnotic pattern_. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast [_vibrant pattern_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1741). Lightweave Scarf (Major) **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 127 **Price** 22,000 gp --- Item bonus when affixed to armor is +3; when affixed to a weapon, creatures are [confused](/Conditions.aspx?ID=63) for 1 minute; and the spell DC is 38. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast 8th-level _vibrant pattern_. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast [_scintillating pattern_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1980).
name: Lightweave Scarf

item_bonus_note: Item bonus to Pick a Lock
markdown:
**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 127 _[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) Greater and major versions of _phantasmal doorknob_ are limited, but the standard version is permitted for use._ **Usage** affixed to armor or weapon --- This ornate doorknob can open doors both material and metaphysical, revealing dreams and nightmares. The spell DC of any spell cast by activating this item is 20.
  • **Armor** You gain a +1 item bonus to [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) checks to [Pick a Lock](/Actions.aspx?ID=2412).
  • **Weapon** If you critically succeed at a [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=1167) with the weapon, the target is [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65) until the end of its next turn.
**Activate** Cast a Spell; **Effect** You cast [_ghost sound_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1528). Phantasmal Doorknob **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 127 **Price** 215 gp --- **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Effect** You cast [_ghost sound_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1528). Phantasmal Doorknob (Greater) **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 127 **Price** 900 gp --- The item bonus when affixed to armor is +2, and the spell DC is 27. On a critical hit with a weapon the doorknob is affixed to, the target is [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=59) until the end of its next turn. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast [_phantasmal treasure_](/Spells.aspx?ID=2013). Phantasmal Doorknob (Major) **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 127 **Price** 6,000 gp --- The item bonus when affixed to armor is +2, and the spell DC is 34. On a critical hit with a weapon the doorknob is affixed to, the target is [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=59) until the end of its next turn. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast 6th-level _phantasmal killer_. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast [_phantasmal calamity_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1630).
name: Phantasmal Doorknob

item_bonus_note: Item bonus to Pick a Lock
markdown:
**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 127 _[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) Greater and major versions of _phantasmal doorknob_ are limited, but the standard version is permitted for use._ **Usage** affixed to armor or weapon --- This ornate doorknob can open doors both material and metaphysical, revealing dreams and nightmares. The spell DC of any spell cast by activating this item is 20.
  • **Armor** You gain a +1 item bonus to [Thievery](/Skills.aspx?ID=50) checks to [Pick a Lock](/Actions.aspx?ID=2412).
  • **Weapon** If you critically succeed at a [Strike](/Actions.aspx?ID=1167) with the weapon, the target is [dazzled](/Conditions.aspx?ID=65) until the end of its next turn.
**Activate** Cast a Spell; **Effect** You cast [_ghost sound_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1528). Phantasmal Doorknob **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 127 **Price** 215 gp --- **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Effect** You cast [_ghost sound_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1528). Phantasmal Doorknob (Greater) **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 127 **Price** 900 gp --- The item bonus when affixed to armor is +2, and the spell DC is 27. On a critical hit with a weapon the doorknob is affixed to, the target is [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=59) until the end of its next turn. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast [_phantasmal treasure_](/Spells.aspx?ID=2013). Phantasmal Doorknob (Major) **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 127 **Price** 6,000 gp --- The item bonus when affixed to armor is +2, and the spell DC is 34. On a critical hit with a weapon the doorknob is affixed to, the target is [blinded](/Conditions.aspx?ID=59) until the end of its next turn. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast 6th-level _phantasmal killer_. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** You cast [_phantasmal calamity_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1630).
name: Phantasmal Doorknob (Major)

item_bonus_note: Item bonus made to Perform when using the staff as an instrument
markdown:
**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 131 **Usage** held in 1 hand **Bulk** 1 --- A composer staff is short and shaped like an elegant black _conductor’s baton_ with a silver tip. When waved through the air, it hums melodically. In this way, you can play it as though it were an instrument, and it grants a +1 item bonus to [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) checks made with it. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Effect** You expend a number of charges from the staff to cast a spell from its list. **Craft Requirements** Supply one casting of all listed levels of all listed spells. Composer Staff **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 131 **Price** 80 gp ---
  • **Cantrip** [_ haunting hymn_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1999), [_summon instrument_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1703)
  • **1st** [_ concordant choir_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1979)
Composer Staff (Greater) **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 132 **Price** 480 gp ---
  • **2nd** [_ sonata span_](/Spells.aspx?ID=998)
  • **3rd** [_ concordant choir_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1979), [_roaring applause_](/Spells.aspx?ID=987), [_sculpt sound_](/Spells.aspx?ID=714)
Composer Staff (Major) **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 132 **Price** 1,800 gp --- The item bonus is +2.
  • **4th** [_ infectious melody_](/Spells.aspx?ID=933)
  • **5th** [_ concordant choir_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1979), [_sculpt sound_](/Spells.aspx?ID=714)
name: Composer Staff

item_bonus_note: Item bonus to Performance checks
markdown:
**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 136 **Usage** held in 2 hands **Bulk** 1 --- While those who appreciate bagpipes may like the sound of this gray reed and black leather instrument, its real purpose is to sow turmoil against a performer’s enemies, spreading discord with each note. While playing the bagpipes, you gain a +1 item bonus to [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) checks and to [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) checks made to [Demoralize](/Actions.aspx?ID=2395). **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Effect** You expend a number of charges from this instrument to cast a spell from its list. **Craft Requirements** Supply one casting of all listed levels of all listed spells. Bagpipes of Turmoil **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 136 **Price** 90 gp ---
  • **Cantrip** [_daze_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1482)
  • **1st** [_bane_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1447)
Bagpipes of Turmoil (Greater) **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 136 **Price** 460 gp ---
  • **2nd** [_deafness_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1483), [_knock_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1581), [_shatter_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1670)
  • **3rd** [_agonizing despair_](/Spells.aspx?ID=665), [_paralyze_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1622)
Bagpipes of Turmoil (Major) **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 136 **Price** 1,900 gp --- The item bonuses are +2.
  • **4th** _confusion_, [_painful vibrations_](/Spells.aspx?ID=964)
  • **5th** [_banishment_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1448), [_crushing despair_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1757), [_synaptic pulse_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1710)
name: Bagpipes of Turmoil

item_bonus_note: Item bonus to Performance
markdown:
**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 136 **Usage** held in 2 hands **Bulk** 1 --- This handheld snare drum is adorned with garish scenes of battle and triumph. When played, no matter what rhythm, it always gives the impression of a marching beat, invoking armies on the move. While playing the drums, you gain a +1 item bonus to [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) checks and a +5-foot status bonus to your Speed. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Effect** You expend a number of charges from this instrument to cast a spell from its list. **Craft Requirements** Supply one casting of all listed levels of all listed spells. Drums of War **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 136 **Price** 90 gp ---
  • **Cantrip** [_shield_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1671)
  • **1st** [_mage armor_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1611), [_magic missile_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1536), [_true strike_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1709)
Drums of War (Greater) **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 136 **Price** 460 gp ---
  • 2nd** ** [_false life_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1523), [_sound burst_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1616), [_spiritual weapon_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1687)
  • **3rd** [_haste_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1553), [_heroism_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1555)
Drums of War (Major) **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 136 **Price** 1,900 gp --- The item bonus to [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) checks is +2.
  • **4th** [_spell immunity_](/Spells.aspx?ID=296), [_tortoise and the hare_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1021)
  • **5th** [_death ward_](/Spells.aspx?ID=64), [_quicken time_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1187), _spell immunity_
name: Drums of War

item_bonus_note: Item bonus to Performance while playing the lute
markdown:
**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 136 **Usage** held in 2 hands **Bulk** L --- This lustrous lute has a polished body that changes to show whatever decorations or carvings you find most appealing, or which best reinforce the story of the song you’re currently playing (as a free action). Its frets are inlaid with mother of pearl. With its mix of enchantment and illusion magic, it’s favored by many traveling minstrels. While playing the lute, you gain a +1 item bonus to [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) and [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) checks. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Effect** You expend a number of charges from this instrument to cast a spell from its list. **Craft Requirements** Supply one casting of all listed levels of all listed spells. Entertainer's Lute **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 136 **Price** 90 gp ---
  • **Cantrip** [_infectious enthusiasm_](/Spells.aspx?ID=932)
  • **1st** [_bless_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1451), [_ventriloquism_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1740)
Entertainer's Lute (Greater) **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 136 **Price** 460 gp ---
  • **2nd** [_calm emotions_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1458), [_mirror image_](/Spells.aspx?ID=197)
  • **3rd** [_enthrall_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1516), [_heroism_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1555), [_illusory creature_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1567)
Entertainer's Lute (Major) **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 136 **Price** 1,900 gp --- The item bonuses are +2.
  • **4th** [_glibness_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1558), [_infectious melody_](/Spells.aspx?ID=933)
  • **5th** [_hallucination_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1551), [_illusory creature_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1567), [_illusory scene_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1570), [_suggestion_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1693)
name: Entertainer's Lute

item_bonus_note: Item bonus to Performance checks
markdown:
**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 136 **Price** 10,000 gp **Usage** held in 2 hands **Bulk** L --- This exquisite fiddle is perfectly carved and balanced to produce the purest sound while granting its player perfect balance and poise. It grants a +2 item bonus to [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) checks. If you’re a master in Performance, while playing it you also gain a +1 status bonus to Reflex saves and a +1 item bonus to Dexterity-based skill checks. If you’re legendary in Performance, the bonuses are +2. When you Perform with the fiddle, you can choose to create a harsh and discordant note. You critically fail the Performance check, shred the strings of the fiddle, and create an 8th-level [_sound burst_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1616), with a 40-foot [emanation](/Rules.aspx?ID=2387) around you instead of a 10-foot [burst](/Rules.aspx?ID=2385). The fiddle can’t be played again until the strings are replaced at a cost of 200 gp. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Effect** You expend a number of charges from this instrument to cast a spell from its list.
  • **Cantrips** [_dancing lights_](/Spells.aspx?ID=58), [_ghost sound_](/Spells.aspx?ID=132), [_message_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1598)
  • **1st** [_command_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1470)
  • **2nd** [_calm emotions_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1458), [_faerie fire_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1653)
  • **3rd** [_enthrall_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1516)
  • **4th** [_glibness_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1558)
  • **5th** [_sending_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1665)
  • **6th** _calm emotions_, [_dominate_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1501), [_vibrant pattern_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1741)
  • **7th** _dominate_, [_prismatic spray_](/Spells.aspx?ID=233), [_true target_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1726), _vibrant pattern_, [_visions of danger_](/Spells.aspx?ID=2040)
**Craft Requirements** Supply one casting of all listed levels of all listed spells.
name: Fiddle of the Maestro

markdown:
**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 137 **Usage** held in 2 hands **Bulk** L --- This ivory flute is adorned with many carvings of eyes. Each has jade pupils and a semiprecious stone as its iris. The eyes flutter open and closed when the flute is played, as if it held the eyes of many independent beings. While playing the flute, you gain a +1 item bonus to [Perception](/Rules.aspx?ID=3303) checks and [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) checks. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Effect** You expend a number of charges from this instrument to cast a spell from its list. **Craft Requirements** Supply one casting of all listed levels of all listed spells. Seer's Flute **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 137 **Price** 90 gp ---
  • **Cantrip** [_guidance_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1549), [_read aura_ ](/Spells.aspx?ID=1646)
  • **1st** [_detect alignment_](/Spells.aspx?ID=65)
Seer's Flute (Greater) **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 137 **Price** 460 gp ---
  • **2nd** [_augury_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1445), [_see invisibility_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1663)
  • **3rd** [_clairaudience_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1465), [_hypercognition_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1563), [_wanderer’s guide_](/Spells.aspx?ID=2043)
Seer's Flute (Major) **Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 137 **Price** 1,900 gp --- The item bonuses are +2.
  • **4th** _clairvoyance_, [_read omens_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1647)
  • **5th** [_inevitable disaster_](/Spells.aspx?ID=930), [_prying eye_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1661), [_tongues_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1728)
name: Seer's Flute

markdown:
**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 137 **Usage** held in 2 hands **Bulk** L --- This ivory flute is adorned with many carvings of eyes. Each has jade pupils and a semiprecious stone as its iris. The eyes flutter open and closed when the flute is played, as if it held the eyes of many independent beings. While playing the flute, you gain a +1 item bonus to [Perception](/Rules.aspx?ID=3303) checks and [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) checks. **Activate** Cast a Spell; **Effect** You expend a number of charges from this instrument to cast a spell from its list. **Craft Requirements** Supply one casting of all listed levels of all listed spells. Common among brutes who use the magic to scare others into compliance, menacing runes lend you a formidable appearance, granting you a +1 item bonus to [Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=40) checks to [Coerce](/Actions.aspx?ID=2394) others.

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 6"
    pfs="Limited"
>
[Vaultbreaker's Harness](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4839)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Transmutation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=157" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 145

**Price** 230 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn backpack

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A _vaultbreaker’s harness_ has four pockets across the chest. The pockets contain a set of [infiltrator’s thieves’ tools](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2758), infiltrator picks, a levered crowbar, and a glass cutter. These items are magically bound to the harness; if they are more than 1 foot away from you, they disappear, then reappear in the harness at the next sunset. Broken or destroyed items similarly reappear, restored, in their proper pockets at sunset.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> Interact;     **Effect** You cinch the harness to prepare for mischief. You gain a +1 item bonus to [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) checks and a +10-foot item bonus to your Speed for 1 minute.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Vaultbreaker's Harness

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 8+"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Clarity Goggles](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4846)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 146

_[**PFS Note**](/pathfinder-society) The counteract check from a pair of _clarity goggles_ has a counteract rank of half the item’s level rounded up._

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn eyepiece

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

_Clarity goggles_ feature faceted lenses that filter your surroundings from several slightly different angles at once, giving you a sharper picture of them. While wearing the goggles, you gain a +1 item bonus to visual [Perception](/Rules.aspx?ID=2040) checks.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> command, envision;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You focus on your environment and the creatures around you to see them as they really are. The GM rolls a secret [counteract](/Rules.aspx?ID=2250) check using your Perception bonus against any [illusion](/Traits.aspx?ID=629) effect created by a 3rd-level or lower spell or a creature of 8th level or lower. You must be able to see the illusion, and it must be within 60 feet. If the check succeeds, you see through the illusion for 10 minutes.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

right="Item 8"
pfs="Standard"
>

</title>

<traits>

</traits>

**Price** 425 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

right="Item 13"
pfs="Standard"
>

</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 147

**Price** 2,450 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The goggles grant a +2 item bonus. They work against effects created by 6th-level or lower spells or a creature of 13th level or lower.

<title
level="2"
right="Item 19"
pfs="Standard"
>
Clarity Goggles (Greater)
</title>

<traits>

</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 147

**Price** 32,500 gp

<row gap="medium">

</row>

</column>

---

The goggles grant a +3 item bonus. They work against effects created by 9th-level or lower spells or a creature of 19th level or lower.
name: Clarity Goggles (Lesser)

<column gap="tiny">

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn eyepiece

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

These goggles feature highly reflective lenses. While wearing the goggles, you gain a +1 item bonus to visual [Perception](/Rules.aspx?ID=2040) checks and to saving throws against [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727) effects.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> Interact;   **Trigger** A creature within 60 feet targets you with a [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727) effect;  **Effect** You turn your head to reflect aspects of the triggering effect back at its creator. The creature must attempt a DC 20 Fortitude save as it becomes disoriented by this reflection. On a failure, the creature is [sickened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=91) 1 (sickened 2 on a critical failure). The creature is temporarily immune for 1 hour.

</column>

Anything viewed through these thin spectacles looks crisp and clear, and the earpieces accentuate sounds around you. You gain a +2 item bonus to [Perception](/Rules.aspx?ID=2040) checks.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> envision;  **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** The spectacles key in on someone to show you their social cues in perfect clarity. Choose a creature you can see. You gain a +3 item bonus on [Perception](/Rules.aspx?ID=2040) checks you make to use [Sense Motive](/Actions.aspx?ID=2302) against that creature. This benefit lasts until you Activate the item again in this way. 

If you’re an [investigator](/Classes.aspx?ID=59), you can use this activation as part of the action you take to [Devise a Stratagem](/Actions.aspx?ID=2813) or [Pursue a Lead](/Actions.aspx?ID=2811), and you can do the latter even though you can’t see the creature. You must choose the same creature you chose for Devise a Stratagem or Pursue a Lead.

**Craft Requirements** You are an [investigator](/Classes.aspx?ID=59).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Spectacles of Inquiry

    pfs="Standard"
>
[Spectacles of Piercing Sight](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4849)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 147

**Price** 24,000 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn eyepiece

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

With lenses set in a silver frame, spectacles of piercing sight grant you a +3 item bonus to [visual](/Traits.aspx?ID=727) Perception checks.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> command;  **Frequency** once per hour;   **Effect** You can see into and through solid matter for 1 minute. This vision can pierce through solid materials up to 20 feet away as if looking at something in normal light even if no illumination is available. You can see through up to 1 foot of stone, 1 inch of metal, or 3 feet of wood or dirt. A thin sheet of lead blocks this vision entirely.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Spectacles of Piercing Sight

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 11"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Accompaniment Cloak](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4850)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Focused" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=74" />
<trait label="Illusion" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=92" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Occult" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=120" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 147

**Price** 1,300 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn cloak

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

This lush velvet capelet is embroidered with images of musicians playing a wide variety of instruments. The images animate when you make art, remaining embroidered, but smoothly unknitting and reknitting in time with your performance. The figures play music that perfectly accompanies your instrument, voice, or movements, granting you a +2 item bonus to [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) checks.

**Activate** <actions string="Free Action" /> envision;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You gain 1 Focus Point, which you can use only to cast a [bard](/Classes.aspx?ID=32) [composition](/Traits.aspx?ID=559) spell. If not used by the end of your turn, this Focus Point is lost.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> envision, Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You peel the musicians from the garment and fling them around you. The cloak casts a 4th-level [_phantom crowd_](/Spells.aspx?ID=968) spell (DC 28); each of the 10‑foot squares must be adjacent to you. The crowd looks like the musicians on the garment and continues to accompany your [Performance](/Skills.aspx?ID=45) checks. You can [Sustain](/Actions.aspx?ID=2317) this effect as described in the spell.

**Craft Requirements** You are a [bard](/Classes.aspx?ID=32).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Accompaniment Cloak

[Cloister Robe](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4853)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Abjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=2" />
<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=48" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 148

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn garment

**Bulk** 1
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The most devoted, [cloistered ](/Doctrines.aspx?ID=4) [clerics](/Classes.aspx?ID=33) wear a cloister robe. Decorations symbolic of a specific deity adorn the robe, and the robe’s colors and the complexity of its construction fit the deity’s outlook. The robe serves as a religious symbol of that deity, and it doesn’t need to be wielded to provide that benefit. 

The robe is _[+1 resilient](/Equipment.aspx?ID=2804) [explorer’s clothing](/Armor.aspx?ID=39)_ and grants a +1 item bonus to [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) checks. The robe doesn’t grant any benefits to a wearer who doesn’t worship the deity tied to the robe. In addition, when you cast a domain spell from one of the deity’s domains, you gain resistance to damage from divine spells until the end of your next turn. This resistance is equal to half the robe’s level.

**Craft Requirements** You are a [cleric](/Classes.aspx?ID=33) who worships the deity tied to the robe.

item_bonus_note: Item bonus to Diplomacy checks while interacting with Dragons
markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 11"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Dragon Rune Bracelet](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4854)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Enchantment" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=61" />
<trait label="Focused" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=74" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 148

**Price** 1,400 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A _dragon rune bracelet_ is a gold bangle formed around the scale of a famous dragon. The bracelet is etched with esoteric symbols or words in [Draconic](/Languages.aspx?ID=156) that indicate kinship with dragons. As many types of _dragon rune bracelet_ exist as there are types of dragons, though bracelets associated with uncommon or rare dragons have the same rarity as the dragon. 

While wearing a dragon rune bracelet, you gain a +2 item bonus to [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) checks while interacting with dragons of the same type as the bracelet’s scale (such as [red dragons](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=582) or [cloud dragons](/MonsterFamilies.aspx?ID=637)). You also gain a +2 item bonus to saving throws against [fear](/Traits.aspx?ID=598) effects from any kind of dragon.

**Activate** <actions string="Free Action" /> envision;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You gain 1 Focus Point, which you can spend only to cast a sorcerer draconic bloodline spell. If not used by the end of your turn, this Focus Point is lost.

**Craft Requirements** You are a [sorcerer](/Classes.aspx?ID=62) of a [draconic](/Bloodlines.aspx?ID=23) bloodline.

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 11"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Eidolon Cape](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4855)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Focused" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=74" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 148

**Price** 1,400 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn cloak

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Though it appears to be one elegant piece, an _eidolon cape_ consists of a mantle for your shoulders and a detachable cape. The cape is designed to resemble your eidolon, either with a direct likeness or with features reminiscent of your eidolon. For instance, if your eidolon is a dragon, the cape might depict a stylized dragon, or it might have a pattern of colorful scales with gold trim. An _eidolon cape_ features the sigil you share with your eidolon prominently in its design. You gain a +2 item bonus to the skill that matches your eidolon’s tradition: [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) for [arcane](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=1), [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) for [divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2), [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) for [occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3), or [Nature](/Skills.aspx?ID=43) for [primal](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=4). 

When you [Manifest your Eidolon](/Actions.aspx?ID=757), the cape transforms into the eidolon, and when the eidolon unmanifests, it turns back into the cape. If the cape is attached to the mantle, it comes free immediately, and the eidolon appears adjacent to you as normal. If not, the eidolon appears in a space adjacent to the cape. With the cape detached, the mantle is still light Bulk, and the whole cloak remains invested. If the cape is out of range (beyond 100 feet, typically) or there’s not enough open space for the eidolon to manifest, your attempt to Manifest your Eidolon fails.

**Activate** <actions string="Free Action" /> envision;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You gain 1 Focus Point, which you can use only to cast a summoner link spell. If not used by the end of your turn, this Focus Point is lost.

**Craft Requirements** You are a [summoner](/Classes.aspx?ID=18).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

    level="1"
    right="Item 11"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Ki-Channeling Beads](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4857)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=48" />
<trait label="Focused" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=74" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 150

**Price** 1,300 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

As you move your body, ki flows into ki-channeling beads you wear and have invested, making them one with your life force. In their usual form, beads are spheres of wood, but versions customized to different martial orders are common. You gain a +2 item bonus to [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) checks.

**Activate** <actions string="Free Action" /> envision;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You gain 1 Focus Point, which you can use only to cast a [divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2) [monk](/Classes.aspx?ID=60) ki spell. If not used by the end of your turn, this Focus Point is lost.

**Activate** <actions string="Free Action" /> envision;  **Frequency** once per hour;  **Requirements** You have just [Refocused](/Actions.aspx?ID=2621) by meditating; **Effect** While meditating, you searched your feelings for a portent of the future. You’re affected by an [_augury_](/Spells.aspx?ID=1445) spell.

**Craft Requirements** You are a [monk](/Classes.aspx?ID=60) with [divine](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=2) ki spells.

</column>

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 11"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Mask of Uncanny Breath](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4858)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Focused" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=74" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Necromancy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=117" />
<trait label="Occult" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=120" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 150

**Price** 1,200 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn mask

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A thin wooden mask carved in the shape of a skull, monstrous face, or eerily featureless visage, a mask of uncanny breath fully covers your face. While wearing it, each breath you take feels cool and pure, perfectly flowing to feed your ki. You gain [resistance](/Rules.aspx?ID=2318) 10 to inhaled poisons and can breathe in an airless or toxic environment. When you breathe in, fragments of bizarre knowledge flow through you, granting you a +2 item bonus to [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) checks.

**Activate** <actions string="Free Action" /> envision;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You gain 1 Focus Point, which you can use only to cast an occult monk ki spell. If not used by the end of your turn, this Focus Point is lost.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> command;  **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** Your unarmed Strike hits a creature that breathes;  **Effect** The mask contorts and inhales, sucking breath from your target’s lungs. The target falls [unconscious](/Conditions.aspx?ID=95) but doesn’t fall prone or drop what it’s holding. It wakes up at the end of your turn if it hasn’t been woken up already.

**Craft Requirements** You are a [monk](/Classes.aspx?ID=60) with [occult](/SpellLists.aspx?Tradition=3) ki spells.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>

    pfs="Standard"
>
[Master Magus Ring](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4859)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Arcane" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=11" />
<trait label="Conjuration" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=33" />
<trait label="Focused" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=74" />

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Elegant jewelry such as the _master magus ring_ adorns experienced magi. Each ring has a significant metal and symbol to represent a particular hybrid study, such as a heavy iron ring with an icon of a mountain for [inexorable iron](/HybridStudies.aspx?ID=1), or glittering silver with a shield-like emblem for [sparkling targe](/HybridStudies.aspx?ID=3). You gain a +2 item bonus to [Arcana](/Skills.aspx?ID=35) checks.

**Activate** <actions string="Free Action" /> envision;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You gain 1 Focus Point, which you can use only to cast a magus conflux spell. If not used by the end of your turn, this Focus Point is lost.

**Activate** <actions string="Free Action" /> command;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** The ring transports you and any items you’re wearing and holding from your current space to an unoccupied space you can see within a range equal to your Speed. If this would bring another creature with you—even if you’re carrying it in an extradimensional container—the activation fails and is used.

**Craft Requirements** You are a [magus](/Classes.aspx?ID=17).

</column>

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 11"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Mind's Light Circlet](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4860)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Enchantment" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=61" />
<trait label="Focused" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=74" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Light" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=100" />
<trait label="Occult" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=120" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 150

**Price** 1,200 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn headwear

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Gemstones of many colors adorn the silver of a _mind’s light circlet_. When you’re charged with mental power, the jewels scintillate with light, with different gems resonating based on your emotions. If you have at least 1 Focus Point, the gems cast [dim light](/Rules.aspx?ID=2403) in a 10-foot radius. When you amp a spell, the light increases to bright light in a 20-foot radius (and dim light to the next 20 feet) until the start of your next turn.

You gain a +2 item bonus to [Occultism](/Skills.aspx?ID=44) checks. You also gain the following amp, which you can apply to any of your psi cantrips that have a target or area, much like an amp gained from a feat.

**Amp** You transfer some of the magical luminescence from the _mind’s light circlet_ to one of the creatures. Choose a creature targeted by the spell or in its area. Until the start of your next turn, that creature sheds [bright light](/Rules.aspx?ID=2402) in a 20‑foot radius (and dim light to the next 20 feet) and can’t be [concealed](/Conditions.aspx?ID=62). If the creature is [invisible](/Conditions.aspx?ID=83), it’s concealed while alight, rather than being [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=96).

**Activate** <actions string="Free Action" /> envision;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You gain 1 Focus Point, which you can use only to use a [psychic](/Classes.aspx?ID=21) [amp](/Traits.aspx?ID=425). If not used by the end of your turn, this Focus Point is lost.

**Craft Requirements** You are a [psychic](/Classes.aspx?ID=21).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Mind's Light Circlet

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 11"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Oracular Crown](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4861)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=48" />
<trait label="Focused" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=74" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Necromancy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=117" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 151

**Price** 1,200 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn headwear

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Patterns themed to your curse cover your _oracular crown_. As your curse worsens, the appearance of the crown changes, introducing extreme angles, stronger colors, or other indications of the intensity of your curse. Similarly, it gets closer to its natural form when you reduce the effects of your curse. You gain a +2 item bonus to [Religion](/Skills.aspx?ID=46) checks.

**Activate** <actions string="Free Action" /> envision;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You gain 1 Focus Point, which you can use only to cast an oracle revelation spell. If not used by the end of your turn, this Focus Point is lost.

**Activate** <actions string="Single Action" /> command ([healing](/Traits.aspx?ID=623), [necromancy](/Traits.aspx?ID=117), positive) **Frequency** once per day;  **Requirements** Your oracular curse is at its minor stage or higher; **Effect** You regain 3d8 Hit Points. This amount increases to 5d8 if your curse is at its moderate stage or 7d8 if it’s at its major stage. If you have negative healing, this activation has the negative trait and not the healing and positive traits.

**Craft Requirements** You are an [oracle](/Classes.aspx?ID=61).

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Oracular Crown

**Activate** <actions string="Free Action" /> envision;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You gain 1 Focus Point, which you can use only to cast a [ranger](/Classes.aspx?ID=36) warden spell. When you use this Focus Point, the warden's signet also casts a 4th-level [_barkskin_](/Spells.aspx?ID=20) spell on you. If not used by the end of your turn, this Focus Point is lost.

**Craft Requirements** You are a [ranger](/Classes.aspx?ID=36) with at least one warden spell.

</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Evocation" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=65" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />

<trait label="Sonic" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=147" />

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 151

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn footwear

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Unassuming in appearance, these slippers indicate their nature only with a signature strip of yellow stitching. You gain a +2 item bonus to [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) checks.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> command, envision, Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You move like the wind, with precision and speed. You [Stride](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305) up to 60 feet; this movement doesn’t trigger reactions. When you stop, if you’ve moved at least 30 feet from where you started, you release a thunderous 5-foot emanation that deals 2d6 bludgeoning damage and 2d6 sonic damage with a DC 25 [basic](/Rules.aspx?ID=2297) Fortitude save. A creature that critically fails its save is also knocked [prone](/Conditions.aspx?ID=88).

<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 6"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Crown of the Companion](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4866)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Healing" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=89" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
<trait label="Necromancy" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=117" />
<trait label="Positive" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=128" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 151

**Price** 250 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn headwear

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

Stories tell of a king who once loved his subjects so much he was willing to give his own life energy for them, using an object like the _crown of the companion_. Whether true or not, this majestic wooden crown bears elaborate carvings depicting that tale with images of a regal figure giving increasingly of themself to a throng of needy subjects. While wearing this crown, you gain a +1 item bonus to [Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=39) checks.

**Activate** <actions string="Two Actions" /> envision, Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You bow to an ally within 30 feet, creating a magical bond between the two of you as if you cast [_shield other_](/Spells.aspx?ID=281) targeting the ally. The link remains even if you move more than 30 feet away from them. At the end of the spell's duration, your ally recovers 4d8 Hit Points and you recover half of what they recover.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Crown of the Companion

markdown:
**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 151 **Price** 220 gp **Usage** worn headwear **Bulk** L --- Made of materials scavenged from wild places, a _druid’s crown_ can be rebuilt for a variety of benefits. The crown grants you a +1 item bonus to a skill, and can be activated to cast a spell, both depending on the material used to build the crown, as listed on the table below. If you invest and wear [druid's vestments](/Equipment.aspx?ID=434) along with the crown, the crown's item bonus increases by 1 and its spell's DC rises to 27. You can invest this item only if you're a [druid](/Classes.aspx?ID=34). When you do, as a 10-minute activity that has the [manipulate](/Traits.aspx?ID=645) trait, you can disassemble and rebuild the crown with different materials, changing its item bonus and spell accordingly. ##
**Material****Item Bonus****Spell**
Antlers[Intimidation](/Skills.aspx?ID=7)[_Enlarge_](/Spells.aspx?ID=102)
Flowers[Diplomacy](/Skills.aspx?ID=6)[_Animal allies_](/Spells.aspx?ID=863)
Leaves[Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=15)[_Tree Shape_](/Spells.aspx?ID=342)
 **Activate** envision; **Frequency** once per day; **Effect** The crown casts its spell at 2nd level (DC 20).
name: Druid's Crown

A _helm of underwater action_ is a streamlined sallet with a visor and a flanged rear. While wearing the helmet with the visor down, you can see, hear, and speak clearly underwater. You also have a +1 item bonus to [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) checks to [Swim](/Actions.aspx?ID=2381).

**Activate** <actions string="Three Actions" /> command, envision, Interact;  **Frequency** once per day;   **Effect** You can breathe underwater for 8 hours. During this time, you have a [swim Speed](/Rules.aspx?ID=2351) equal to your land Speed.

summary_markdown: The helm grants a +2 item bonus. Its activation lasts until your next daily preparations.

item_bonus_note: Item bonus to Warfare Lore Checks
markdown:
**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 152 **Price** 160 gp **Usage** worn headwear **Bulk** L --- Repurposing and enchanting a helmet worn by a battlefield commander can create a _tactician’s helm_, imparting knowledge of battlefield tactics that feeds off your minor victories. The helm grants you a +1 item bonus to [Warfare Lore](/Skills.aspx?ID=41) checks. Also, a jewel adorns the brow of the helmet. This jewel becomes charged each time you hit a creature with an Attack of Opportunity. A _tactician’s helm_ can hold up to 2 charges, and its charges reset to 0 when you invest it. **Activate** command; **Frequency** once per hour; **Requirements** The helm’s jewel is charged; **Effect** One charge in the helm’s jewel expires, and you choose one of the following effects.
  • **Charge!** [Stride](/Actions.aspx?ID=2305) twice.
  • **Move It!** You gain a +2 status bonus to [Acrobatics](/Skills.aspx?ID=34) and [Athletics](/Skills.aspx?ID=36) checks until the end of this turn.
  • **Protect!** If you’re wielding a shield, Stride to a space adjacent to an ally, then [Raise your Shield](/Actions.aspx?ID=2316).
  • **Re-Arm!** [Interact](/Actions.aspx?ID=2297) up to three times. Each of these actions must be used to do something on Table 6–2: [Changing Equipment](/Rules.aspx?ID=2151).
name: Tactician's Helm

item_bonus_note: Item bonus to Survival checks
markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 4"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Hunter's Arrowhead](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4875)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Rare" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=137" />
<trait label="Enchantment" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=61" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 153

**Price** 80 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A _hunter’s arrowhead_ is meant to be worn as a charm, such as a pendant, or carried in a pocket or quiver. The arrowhead is etched with images sacred to the elven god [Ketephys](/Deities.aspx?ID=462). While you wear or carry the arrowhead, it infuses you with great skill at hunting, and you gain a +1 item bonus to [Survival](/Skills.aspx?ID=49) checks and attack rolls against any creature you’ve currently designated as your prey with [Hunt Prey](/Actions.aspx?ID=10). A _hunter’s arrowhead_ is also a religious symbol of Ketephys.

**Activate** <actions string="Reaction" /> command;  **Frequency** once per day; **Trigger** You would miss with an attack made with a bow;  **Effect** You gain a +2 circumstance bonus to your attack roll, possibly turning a miss into a hit.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Hunter's Arrowhead

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 6"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Spiritsight Sight](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4878)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
<trait label="Magical" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=103" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 154

**Price** 225 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn

</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

The opal set in the intricately carved ivory _spiritsight ring_ eventually becomes translucent and tickles your finger whenever an [incorporeal](/Traits.aspx?ID=632) creature is nearby. When in the presence of a nearby incorporeal creature, even if it’s within a solid object, you eventually detect the creature, though you might not do so instantly, and you can’t pinpoint the location. This acts as a vague sense, like humans’ sense of smell. An incorporeal creature trying to hide its presence from this sense attempts a [Stealth](/Skills.aspx?ID=48) check against your [Perception](/Rules.aspx?ID=2040) DC to hide from your vague sense, as normal for attempting to foil special senses. You gain a +2 item bonus when using the [Seek](/Actions.aspx?ID=2301) action to find [hidden](/Conditions.aspx?ID=79) or [undetected](/Conditions.aspx?ID=96) incorporeal creatures within 30 feet of you.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Spiritsight Sight

markdown:
<title
    level="1"
    right="Item 3"
    pfs="Standard"
>
[Skinsaw Mask](/Equipment.aspx?ID=4886)
</title>

<traits>
<trait label="Uncommon" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=159" />
<trait label="Divination" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=47" />
<trait label="Divine" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=48" />
<trait label="Evil" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=64" />
<trait label="Invested" url="/Traits.aspx?ID=98" />
</traits>

<column gap="tiny">

**Source** [Treasure Vault](/Sources.aspx?ID=320) pg. 155

**Price** 50 gp

<row gap="medium">

**Usage** worn mask

**Bulk** L
</row>

<row gap="medium">

 

 

 
</row>

</column>

---

A patchwork of humanoid flesh makes up a _skinsaw mask_, which is stitched together with black silk or wire. It is distinctive for its bulbous orange eye—crafted from a magical glass bauble—and wide row of teeth. When worn, the mask amplifies your ability to sense fear in other creatures. You know the value of the [frightened](/Conditions.aspx?ID=76) condition of any observed creature, and you gain a +1 item bonus to [Perception](/Rules.aspx?ID=2040) checks to [Seek](/Actions.aspx?ID=2301) frightened creatures. Whenever you deal precision damage to a frightened creature, you deal 1 additional precision damage. If you aren’t [evil](/Traits.aspx?ID=64), you are [drained](/Conditions.aspx?ID=68) 2 while wearing a _skinsaw mask_.

<column gap="tiny">

</column>
name: Skinsaw Mask
